region_of_origin: Japan (widely consumed in Singapore at Japanese restaurants, izakayas, and available at Don Don Donki and NTUC FairPrice frozen section)
food_subgroup: Plant-based protein — immature soybean (edamame, Glycine max at R6 stage); distinct from mature dry yellow soybean and from tofu/tempeh

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for edamame as a food item; SNOMED CT coverage for specific prepared foods is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of edamame in a Singapore izakaya or Japanese restaurant is approximately 100g of shelled cooked beans, often served as a starter or snack.

nutrient_source.title: Edamame, frozen, prepared — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 11211 / FDC ID 168411)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Edamame, frozen, prepared' (FDC ID 168411). Sodium value (180mg/100g) reflects typical salted-water preparation as served in Singapore restaurants; unsalted edamame contains approximately 5mg sodium per 100g per USDA data. All other macros and micronutrients are from the USDA SR Legacy entry.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Edamame — University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Xiaoli et al.)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI value of 25 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for edamame/immature soybeans. Peer-reviewed study (Xiaoli et al.) reported GI = 18 for edamame; the University of Sydney GI Database consensus value of approximately 25 is used as the reference. GL per 100g serving = GI (25) × available carbohydrate (6g net, after fibre subtraction from 9g total) / 100 = 1.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium (180mg/100g from salted preparation) offset by high potassium (436mg/100g); favourable Na:K ratio
hypertension.guidance: Edamame prepared in salted water contains approximately 180mg sodium per 100g; patients with hypertension should request unsalted edamame (≈5mg Na/100g) or limit portion size, while benefiting from the high potassium content (436mg/100g) which supports blood pressure regulation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low GI (25) and GL (1.5 per 100g serving); high protein (12g) and fibre (5g) slow glucose absorption
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 25 and GL of 1.5 per 100g serving, edamame produces a negligible postprandial glucose response and is an excellent high-protein, high-fibre snack for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; thiamine (0.23mg/100g) also supports metabolic function, which is often compromised in T2DM.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.6g/100g), zero cholesterol, soy isoflavones, and anti-inflammatory omega-3 ALA
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Edamame is low in saturated fat (0.6g/100g), contains no cholesterol, and provides soy isoflavones (genistein, daidzein) and alpha-linolenic acid (ALA omega-3) which have modest LDL-lowering and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a cardioprotective plant protein choice consistent with Singapore HPB dietary guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Exceptionally high folate (311mcg/100g) critical for patients on methotrexate (MTX); anti-inflammatory PUFA and isoflavones
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Edamame provides 311mcg folate per 100g (approximately 78% of daily RDI), which is clinically relevant for RA patients on methotrexate therapy, as MTX inhibits dihydrofolate reductase and depletes cellular folate; dietary folate from edamame is a positive complementary source, though supplemental folic acid (5mg/week as recommended by HPB Singapore) remains necessary.

pregnancy.key_factor: High folate (311mcg/100g) supports neural tube development; high plant protein and iron
pregnancy.guidance: Edamame is an excellent food during pregnancy, providing 311mcg folate per 100g to support neural tube development, 12g plant protein, 2.3mg iron, and 63mg calcium, making it a nutrient-dense snack suitable for pregnant women.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Edamame (cooked) are immature soybeans (Glycine max) harvested at the R6 pod-fill stage, boiled or steamed and typically seasoned with salt. They are a staple Japanese izakaya snack widely available in Singapore at Japanese restaurants, Don Don Donki (fresh and frozen), and NTUC FairPrice (frozen 400g bags). Per 100g of shelled cooked beans: 121 kcal, 12g protein (soy protein, PDCAAS ~0.91 — high biological value), 5g fat (0.6g saturated, ~2.8g polyunsaturated including ALA omega-3), 9g carbohydrate, 5g dietary fibre, 2.2g sugar. Micronutrients: 311mcg folate (78% daily RDI — critically important for pregnancy and for rheumatoid arthritis patients on methotrexate), 436mg potassium, 180mg sodium (salted preparation; ~5mg if unsalted), 63mg calcium, 64mg magnesium, 2.3mg iron, 169mg phosphorus, 1.3mg zinc, 6mg vitamin C, 0.23mg thiamine (B1). GI ~25 (low), GL ~1.5 per 100g serving — negligible glycaemic impact. NOVA Class 1 (unprocessed/minimally processed). Vegan, halal, kosher, gluten-free, dairy-free. Taste: mildly sweet, nutty, slightly grassy. Texture: tender with a slight bite. Distinct from mature yellow soybeans (higher water content, greener colour, sweeter flavour) and from processed soy products (tofu, tempeh, soy milk). Source: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 168411.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 168411, 'Edamame, frozen, prepared') — an approved primary source with exact match to the food described. GI value from University of Sydney GI Database (approved GI source). Ontology codes (FoodOn, FoodEx2) are defensible and verified. All mandatory sections complete.
notes: Cholesterol is confirmed zero (0.0mg/100g) per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — edamame is a plant food and naturally contains no cholesterol. Vitamin B12 is confirmed zero (0.0mcg/100g) — naturally zero as edamame is a plant food with no animal-derived components. Vitamin D is confirmed zero (0.0mcg/100g) — naturally zero for this plant food per USDA data. Trans fat is confirmed zero (0.0g/100g) per USDA SR Legacy. Sodium value of 180mg/100g reflects typical salted-water restaurant preparation in Singapore; home-prepared unsalted edamame contains approximately 5mg sodium per 100g per USDA SR Legacy. Monounsaturated fat (1.1g) and polyunsaturated fat (2.8g) values are from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 168411 and are included to complete the fatty acid profile. Water content (72.8g/100g) from USDA SR Legacy. The Japan MEXT 8th Edition was referenced in the input source citation but is not an approved source under Project Veritas v2; all values are confirmed against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy only. GL calculation: GI (25) × available carbohydrate per serving (6g net, after fibre subtraction from 9g total carbohydrate) / 100 = 1.5 per 100g serving.
